manifest.name: ripgrep - Rust Codebase Triage Map
manifest.description: Analyzes 214 files across Rust, Ruby, and shell scripts to generate semantic metadata for intelligent code navigation. This manifest tracks action-object keywords, functional parent domains, key entities, and logic constraints to enable AI agents to understand file purpose and discover relevant code segments quickly.

analyzers.name: agent-file-triage::file-content::default
analyzers.description: Generates file-local triage metadata that helps AI agents decide which candidate files to open, skip, or investigate next.

fields.name: triage_summary
fields.description: One concise sentence explaining when this file is useful to open during agent navigation.

fields.name: open_when
fields.description: 1-4 concrete task conditions where an agent should open this file.

fields.name: skip_when
fields.description: 1-4 concrete false-positive conditions where an agent should avoid opening this file.

fields.name: answers_questions
fields.description: 1-4 concrete questions this file can answer.

fields.name: context_needed
fields.description: Missing context an agent should search for next if this file is relevant but incomplete. Null if none is visible or needed.

fields.name: related_context
fields.description: Only visibly referenced paths, modules, packages, symbols, commands, routes, or config keys. Null if none are visible.

fields.name: change_risk_reason
fields.description: One sentence explaining the risk using visible evidence.

fields.name: confidence_reason
fields.description: One sentence explaining confidence in the triage metadata using visible evidence.
